name: Alignment of Complex Systems Research Group

overview.description: Interdisciplinary AI safety research group studying multi-agent systems of humans and advanced AI
overview.description_long: The Alignment of Complex Systems Research Group (ACS) is an interdisciplinary research group studying questions about multi-agent systems composed of humans and advanced AI systems. Their goal is to develop an understanding of advanced AI systems and the complex ways in which they are embedded in and interact with humans and human institutions, contributing to making such systems safe and aligned with shared human values. Key research themes include Gradual Disempowerment (systemic existential risks from incremental AI), LLM Psychology and Sociology, Hierarchical Agency, and building a Science of the Space of Intelligent Systems.
overview.tagline: Investigating how complex systems composed of both humans and AIs could be aligned with human values

history.origin_story: Announced in May 2022 as a research group affiliated with the Center for Theoretical Study at Charles University in Prague, focused on AI alignment and safety for complex human-AI systems.

key_milestones.event: Launched InterLab toolkit for multi-agent interaction experiments
Hosted Workshop on Post-AGI Outcomes and Human-Aligned AI Summer School 2025
Contributor to International AI Safety Report 2026

notable_people.name: Theia Pearson-Vogel
notable_people.note: Creator of the repeng open-source library for representation engineering; 2024 New Science fellow

notable_people.name: Annie Stephenson
notable_people.note: PhD in applied physics from Harvard; postdoc at Princeton studying collective behavior in online communities

notable_people.name: Vojtěch Kovařík
notable_people.note: PhD in mathematics from Charles University; postdoc at CMU Foundations of Cooperative AI Lab; research at Oxford Future of Humanity Institute

notable_people.name: Tomáš Gavenčiak
notable_people.note: PhD in theoretical computer science; research in game theory, algorithms, Bayesian modelling

notable_people.name: Gavin Leech
notable_people.note: Cofounded Arb research consultancy; PhD in ML; advised UK government on Covid and AI policy

notable_people.name: Nora Ammann
notable_people.note: Contributor on value malleability and naturalized planning papers

awards: Best poster award at 5th International Workshop on Active Inference (2024)
partnerships: Center for Theoretical Study, Charles University in Prague (academic affiliation)

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: ACS is an AI safety research group whose work focuses on aligning advanced AI systems — AGI progress increases the relevance and urgency of their research rather than displacing it.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Research group operating in academia and the AI safety community; not subject to conventional market dynamics.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: Small research group supported by Epistea (a Czech nonprofit) and affiliated with Charles University. No evidence of large institutional funders (e.g. Open Philanthropy); long-term funding stability is unclear for a group of this size in the nascent AI safety research ecosystem.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: ACS demonstrates strong research output (ICML 2025, PNAS, arXiv papers), a growing international team, and institutional anchoring at Charles University Prague. Their work on gradual disempowerment and LLM psychology occupies a distinctive niche in the AI safety landscape with growing relevance.

metadata.notes: Group founded May 2022. Address: Husova 4, 110 00 Prague. Academic part affiliated with Center for Theoretical Study at Charles University. Supported by Epistea, z.s. Twitter: @acsresearchorg. LinkedIn shows 'ACS Research' with 3 followers. Research Manager Martin Vaněk confirmed via paper co-authorship. Group runs Human-Aligned AI Summer School annually.
